title: Ancient Snake with Legs Found! And It Had a Cheekbone!
summary: Imagine a snake with legs! That sounds like a made-up creature, but it was real. Scientists found a fossil of a snake called Najash rionegrina that lived nearly 100 million years ago. It had hind legs and a cheekbone (a bone in the face) that most snakes today don't have. The fossil was discovered in Argentina, and scientists used a special X-ray machine called micro-CT to see inside it without breaking it. What they found surprised them. For a long time, people thought snakes started as small animals that burrowed underground. But this fossil shows that early snakes were big-bodied and had wide mouths. They also kept their hind legs for a long time before losing them. "Our findings support the idea that the ancestors of modern snakes were big-bodied and big-mouthed," said Fernando Garberoglio, the lead scientist. This discovery helps us understand how snakes evolved from lizards into the legless creatures we see today. Pretty cool, right?
why_it_matters: This discovery shows how scientists piece together the past, just like you learn about history from old stories and objects.

keywords.explanation: The scientific name of the ancient snake found in the fossil.
Remains of a living thing from long ago, preserved in rock.
A bone in the face that most modern snakes don't have.
The country in South America where the fossil was found.
A special X-ray machine that lets scientists see inside fossils without breaking them.
Having a large body, unlike the small burrowing snakes scientists once thought ancestors were.

questions.question: Who is the lead scientist mentioned?

background_read: Snakes are reptiles that evolved from lizards many millions of years ago.
Some ancient lizards had legs, but over time some lost their legs and became snakes.
Finding fossils with legs helps scientists draw the family tree of snakes.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists led by Fernando Garberoglio
WHAT: Discovered a fossil of a snake called Najash rionegrina that had hind legs and a cheekbone
WHY: To understand how snakes evolved from lizards into legless animals

perspectives.description: They are excited because the fossil gives new clues about how snakes changed over time.
They might think it's cool to imagine a snake with legs and learn how animals evolve.
